notes: Uplay install runs and then game intro video runs till the starting screen and crashes.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes:
With the latest version of Proton Uplay is fixed so the game launches. The intro videos play just fine and the game works up to the "Press a key to go to the main menu" prompt, when you press a key and it start to load the main menu that is when issues occur. When the main menu loads and it does it's online validation the game suddenly closes, I am suspecting this is caused by DRM which force closes the game once it realizes it's not running on Windows. Sometimes it does get past this point and loads in to the main menu before closing the game a few seconds later. 

So basically; game runs fine, but DRM prevents you from playing it.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes: Games launches, loads main menu (connects online services?) then closes. Cannot play, borked.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes: Got past the Connecting to Online Services on the first boot, even got to the loading screen, but as soon as the game loads it crashes, after the first boot, the game crashes after Connecting to Online Services.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes: Loads but crashes after main menu loads.  Saw some notes about disabling nvapi (https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2464) but that doesn't work on my system.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes:
The Internet connection needs to be disabled before accessing the main menu, otherwise the game will crash. It can be enabled again after launching the game.

It runs very well for the most part, but has graphical glitches in arena fights for example.

In case Uplay refuses to start, my workaround is to delete the proton profile and let steam re-create it. Also all the notification settings in Uplay should be turned off for a better experience.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes:
As the previous poster mentions, the game requires that the Internet connection be disabled before the main menu. As the game relies on Uplay (which requires an Internet connection to initialise the game) this means carefully timing the network disable after uplay loads while the nvidia and other intro videos are playing but before the main menu loads.  Is a hosts file or iptables block possible for this specific connection?
Using FXAA in combination with MSAAx2 or MSAAx4 causes visual errors, with strips of inverted colour across the image.
Performance appears solid at high resolutions.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes: Works well when "always start Uplay in offline mode" is selected in uplay settings/network.  Do not attempt to run FXAA+MSAAx2 or FXAA+MSAAx4 anti-aliasing modes as these cause graphical artifacts.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes: Runs after setting "always start Uplay in offline mode", but a low fps.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes: Turn off your internet access to reach the game. It's playable for awhile but crashes right after.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes:
Runs pretty well after launching the Uplay client in offline mode.
I had pretty stable ~60 FPS on Ultra High without using MXAA.
I give it just a silver rating because for some gamers the achievement are very important, and you can't get them, if you play offline.
title: Assassin's Creed® Syndicate

notes: The game runs fine and without any issues once Uplay is set to start in offline mode. Proprietary DRM garbage closes the game once it makes a connection to the internet..

notes: Runs really well. I give it a gold rating instead of platinum because turning on MSAA provokes graphical glitches. Still, the game looks great ven without MSAA.
